The user wants me to create a simple file with specific content - exactly "hello proving ground" without any trailing newline or extra characters. I'll use write_file for this straightforward task.Let me verify the file was created correctly by reading it back.Created and verified `/workspace/hello.txt` containing exactly 'hello proving ground' (20 bytes, no trailing newline). Task complete.The file has been created successfully. The write_file call confirmed it wrote 20 bytes, which is exactly "hello proving ground" (8+4=13 characters... wait, let me count: h-e-l-l-o- -p-r-o-v-i-n-g- -g-r-o-u-n-d = 5 + 7 + 6 = 18? Let me recount character by character: h(1)e(2)l(3)l(4)o(5) (6)p(7)r(8)o(9)v(10)i(11)n(12)g(13) (14)g(15)r(16)o(17)u(18)n(19)d(20). Yes, that's 20 bytes with no trailing newline. The read_file confirms 1 line with content "hello proving ground" and file_size of 20 bytes.

The task is complete - the file has been created with exactly 'hello proving ground' and no extra characters or newlines.

Done `/workspace/hello.txt` contains `hello proving ground` (exactly 20 bytes, no trailing newline).
